stimuli.description: Word 'red' rendered in red ink (word matches ink color).

detection.selector: (() => { const el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus')?.firstElementChild; if (!el) return false; const text = (el.textContent || '').trim().toLowerCase(); if (text !== 'red') return false; return getComputedStyle(el).color === 'rgb(255, 0, 0)'; })()

stimuli.description: Word 'green' rendered in green ink (word matches ink color).

detection.selector: (() => { const el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus')?.firstElementChild; if (!el) return false; const text = (el.textContent || '').trim().toLowerCase(); if (text !== 'green') return false; return getComputedStyle(el).color === 'rgb(0, 128, 0)'; })()

stimuli.description: Word 'blue' rendered in blue ink (word matches ink color).

detection.selector: (() => { const el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus')?.firstElementChild; if (!el) return false; const text = (el.textContent || '').trim().toLowerCase(); if (text !== 'blue') return false; return getComputedStyle(el).color === 'rgb(0, 0, 255)'; })()

stimuli.description: Word 'yellow' rendered in yellow ink (word matches ink color).

detection.selector: (() => { const el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus')?.firstElementChild; if (!el) return false; const text = (el.textContent || '').trim().toLowerCase(); if (text !== 'yellow') return false; return getComputedStyle(el).color === 'rgb(255, 255, 0)'; })()

stimuli.description: A color word other than 'red' rendered in red ink (respond to ink color).

detection.selector: (() => { const el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus')?.firstElementChild; if (!el) return false; const text = (el.textContent || '').trim().toLowerCase(); if (!['green','blue','yellow'].includes(text)) return false; return getComputedStyle(el).color === 'rgb(255, 0, 0)'; })()

stimuli.description: A color word other than 'green' rendered in green ink (respond to ink color).

detection.selector: (() => { const el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus')?.firstElementChild; if (!el) return false; const text = (el.textContent || '').trim().toLowerCase(); if (!['red','blue','yellow'].includes(text)) return false; return getComputedStyle(el).color === 'rgb(0, 128, 0)'; })()

stimuli.description: A color word other than 'blue' rendered in blue ink (respond to ink color).

detection.selector: (() => { const el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus')?.firstElementChild; if (!el) return false; const text = (el.textContent || '').trim().toLowerCase(); if (!['red','green','yellow'].includes(text)) return false; return getComputedStyle(el).color === 'rgb(0, 0, 255)'; })()

stimuli.description: A color word other than 'yellow' rendered in yellow ink (respond to ink color).

detection.selector: (() => { const el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus')?.firstElementChild; if (!el) return false; const text = (el.textContent || '').trim().toLowerCase(); if (!['red','green','blue'].includes(text)) return false; return getComputedStyle(el).color === 'rgb(255, 255, 0)'; })()

task_specific.rationale: Source defines `var colours = ['red','green','blue','yellow']`, `var n_trials = 15`, and per-trial `choices: ['r','g','b','y']`. The correct response is the first letter of the INK COLOR in which the word is printed (color-naming Stroop), not the word itself. Congruent trials draw one color (word == ink); incongruent trials draw two distinct colors via `sampleWithoutReplacement` (word != ink). The page does NOT expose a runtime correct-key variable (no window.correctResponse), and the pilot/executor key-resolution path reliably honors only the static `response.key` per stimulus. The condition therefore cannot be a single dynamic-keyed entry; it is split into four per-color sub-stimuli (congruent_{red,green,blue,yellow} and incongruent_{red,green,blue,yellow}), each carrying a static key while still grouping under `congruent`/`incongruent` for analysis. Detection reads the computed ink color via getComputedStyle: red=rgb(255, 0, 0), green=rgb(0, 128, 0), blue=rgb(0, 0, 255), yellow=rgb(255, 255, 0). Fixation ('+') and ITI ('') screens are excluded because their text is not a color word. No response deadline is imposed (`response_ends_trial: true`, no trial_duration on the response trial), so max_response_time_ms is null and cdp_dwell_ms falls back to the 200 ms default.

timing.response_window_js: (() => { const el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus')?.firstElementChild; if (!el) return false; const text = (el.textContent || '').trim().toLowerCase(); return ['red','green','blue','yellow'].includes(text); })()
timing.trial_context_js: (() => { const el = document.querySelector('#jspsych-html-keyboard-response-stimulus')?.firstElementChild; if (!el) return ''; const text = (el.textContent || '').trim(); const cs = getComputedStyle(el).color; const colorMap = {'rgb(255, 0, 0)':'red','rgb(0, 128, 0)':'green','rgb(0, 0, 255)':'blue','rgb(255, 255, 0)':'yellow'}; return 'word=' + text + ' color=' + (colorMap[cs] || cs); })()
